Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Agitation, excitement, raging. From ragaz; commotion, restlessness, crash, disquiet, anger.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.

1: From 'el and paz; God of gold; Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau.
Inhab. of Teman. Patronymically from Teyman; a Temanite or descendant of Teman.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: To test, try. A primitive root; to test; by implication, to attempt.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To be weary or impatient. A primitive root; to tire; to be disgusted.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
To restrain, retain. A primitive root; to inclose; by analogy, to hold back; also to maintain, rule, assemble.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
3: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To discipline, chasten, admonish. A primitive root; to chastise, literally or figuratively; hence, to instruct.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
An Israelite in the line of Saul. From raphah; slack.
4: A word, speech, utterance. From malal
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To stumble, stagger, totter. A primitive root; to totter or waver; by implication, to falter, stumble, faint or fall.
To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.
The knee. From barak; a knee.
To bow down. A primitive root; to bend the knee; by implication, to sink, to prostrate.
5: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
To be weary or impatient. A primitive root; to tire; to be disgusted.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
6: A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Stupidity, confidence. Feminine of kecel; in a good sense, trust; in a bad one, silliness.
From tamam; completeness; figuratively, prosperity; usually innocence.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
7: Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Where?. From 'ay and poh; what place?; also when?; or how?.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
8: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
To sow, scatter seed. A primitive root; to sow; figuratively, to disseminate, plant, fructify.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
To dock off, curtail, to harvest. A primitive root; to dock off, i.e. Curtail; especially to harvest.
9: Breath. From nasham; a puff, i.e. Wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. Or an animal.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
10: A lion. Or earyeh; from 'arah; a lion.
A roaring. From sha'ag; a rumbling or moan.
A lion. From an unused root probably meaning to roar; a lion.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
Young lion. From kaphar; a village; also a young lion.
To break, break down, or out. For nathats; to tear out.
11: A lion. From luwsh in the sense of crushing; a lion.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To divide. A primitive root; to break through, i.e. Spread or separate.
12: Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To steal. A primitive root; to thieve; by implication, to deceive.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
A whisper. From an unused root meaning to emit a sound; an inkling.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
13: Divided, a sentiment. Or samiph; from ca'aph; divided, i.e. a sentiment.
Vision. From chazah; a revelation, expectation by dream.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
Deep sleep. From radam; a lethargy or trance.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
14: A trembling. Or radah; from ra'ad; a shudder.
To encounter, befall. A primitive root: to encounter, whether accidentally or in a hostile manner.
Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
To dread, be in dread or in awe. A primitive root; to be startled; hence, to fear in general.
15: Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
To pass on or away, pass through. A primitive root; properly, to slide by, i.e. to hasten away, pass on, spring up, pierce or change.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To bristle up. A primitive root; to be erect, i.e. Bristle as hair.
16: To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Sight, appearance, vision. From ra'ah; a view; also an appearance, whether a shape, or a vision.
Likeness, form. Or tmunah; from miyn; something portioned out, as a shape, i.e. phantom, or embodiment, or manifestation.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A whisper. Feminine from damam; quiet.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
17: Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
To be clean or pure. A primitive root; properly, to be bright; i.e. to be pure.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
18: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A messenger. From an unused root meaning to despatch as a deputy; a messenger; specifically, of God, i.e. An angel.
Error. Feminine of an unused noun meaning bluster; braggadocio, i.e. fatuity.
19: Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
From chamar; properly, a bubbling up, i.e. Of water, a wave; of earth, mire or clay; also a heap; hence, a chomer or dry measure.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Foundation, base. From yacad; a foundation.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A moth. From ashesh; a moth.
20: To beat, crush by beating. A primitive root; to bruise or violently strike.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
21: From yathar; properly, an overhanging, i.e. an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To pull out or up, set out, journey. A primitive root; properly, to pull up, especially the tent-pins, i.e. Start on a journey.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

1: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
To turn. A primitive root; to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. Appear, look, etc.
2: Vexation, anger. Or kaoas; from ka'ac; vexation.
To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.
Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
A primitive root; to open, i.e. Be roomy; usually figuratively to be simple or delude.
3: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Foolish. From an unused root; silly.
To deal with the roots. A primitive root; to root, i.e. Strike into the soil, or to pluck from it.
Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
To utter a curse against, curse. A primitive root; to scoop out, i.e. to malign or execrate.
4: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To be or become far or distant. A primitive root; to widen, i.e. recede or remove.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
5: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
From qatsar; severed, i.e. Harvest, the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Perhaps a thorn, barb. From an unused root meaning to be prickly; a thorn; hence, a cactus-hedge.
Perhaps a snare, trap. From the same as tsammah; a noose; figuratively, destruction.
A primitive root; to inhale eagerly; figuratively, to cover; by implication, to be angry; also to hasten.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
6: From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To sprout, spring up. A primitive root; to sprout.
7: Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Flame. From saraph; a live coal; by analogy lightning; figuratively, an arrow,; specifically, fever.
To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.
To soar, be lofty, to be haughty. A primitive root; to soar, i.e. Be lofty; figuratively, to be haughty.
8: But, but indeed. Apparently a variation of 'uwlay; however or on the contrary.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A cause, reason, manner. Feminine of dabar; a reason, suit or style.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
9: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
A searching, a thing (to be) searched out. From chaqar; examination, enumeration, deliberation.
A primitive root; properly, perhaps to separate, i.e. Distinguish; by implication, to be great, difficult, wonderful.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
10: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
11: To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Low. From shaphel; depressed, literally or figuratively.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
To be dark. A primitive root; to be ashy, i.e. Dark-colored; by implication, to mourn.
A primitive root; to be lofty, especially inaccessible; by implication, safe, strong; used literally and figuratively.
Deliverance, rescue, salvation, safety, welfare. Or yeshai; from yasha'; liberty, deliverance, prosperity.
12: To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
Thought, device. Or machashebeth; from chashab; a contrivance, i.e. a texture, machine, or intention, plan.
Crafty, shrewd, sensible. Passive participle of aram; cunning.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or tushiyah; from an unused root probably meaning to substantiate; support or ability, i.e. help, an undertaking, understanding.
13: To capture, seize, take. A primitive root; to catch; generally, to capture or occupy; also to choose; figuratively, to cohere.
To be shrewd or crafty. A primitive root; properly, to be bare; but used only in the derivative sense to be cunning.
To hasten. A primitive root; properly, to be liquid or flow easily, i.e. to hurry; often used adverbially, promptly.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
To twist. A primitive root; to twine, i.e. to struggle or be tortuous.
14: To meet, encounter. A primitive root; to come in contact with, whether by accident or violence; figuratively, to concur.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
Daytime, by day. From yowm; daily.
To feel, grope. A primitive root; to feel of; by implication, to grope.
A light, double light, noon. From tsahar; a light: dual double light, i.e. Noon.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
15: To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Strong, stout, mighty. From chazaq; strong.
16: Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
Draw together, shut. A primitive root; to draw together, i.e. Close; by implication, to leap; specifically, to die.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
17: Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To spurn, to disappear. A primitive root; to spurn; also to disappear.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
18: To be in pain. A primitive root; properly, to feel pain; by implication, to grieve; figuratively, to spoil.
To bind, bind on, bind up. A primitive root; to wrap firmly; figuratively, to stop, to rule.
A primitive root; to dash asunder; by implication, to crush, smash or violently plunge; figuratively, to subdue or destroy.
To heal. Or raphah; a primitive root; properly, to mend, i.e. to cure.
19: To strip, plunder, deliver oneself, be delivered, snatch away, deliver.
Masculine shishshah; a primitive number; six (see suws) beyond five or the fingers of the hand); as ord. Sixth.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
Or shibrah; from shaba'; a primitive cardinal number; seven; also seven times; by implication, a week; by extension, an indefinite number.
20: To ransom. A primitive root; to sever, i.e. Ransom; gener. To release, preserve.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
A battle, war. From lacham; a battle; generally, war.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
21: To withdraw, hide. A primitive root; to secrete.
A scourge, whip. From shuwt; a lash.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Violence, havoc, devastation, ruin. Or showd; from shuwd; violence, ravage.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
22: To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
Violence, havoc, devastation, ruin. Or showd; from shuwd; violence, ravage.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
23: A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.

Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.

25: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.

Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Issue, offspring, produce. From yatsa'; issue, i.e. Produce, children.
Herb, herbage. From an unused root meaning to glisten; grass.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

A grave, sepulcher. Or qibrah; from qabar; a sepulchre.
Firm or rugged strength. From an unused root meaning to be complete; maturity.
A stack of sheaves, a tomb. From an unused root; a stack of sheaves; by analogy, a tomb.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
27: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
To search. A primitive root; properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately.

So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.

Vexation, anger. Or kaoas; from ka'ac; vexation.

The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Terrors, alarms. Masculine plural from ba'ath; alarms.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To set in a, row, arrange, put in order. A primitive root; to set in a row, i.e. Arrange, put in order.
5: A wild donkey. Or pereh; from para' in the secondary sense of running wild; the onager.
To bray, cry. A primitive root; to bray, scream
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Grass. From dasha; a sprout; by analogy, grass.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A head of cattle (bullock, ox, etc.). From shuwr; a bullock.
To low. A primitive root; to bellow.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Fodder. From balal; mixed, i.e. feed.

To eat. A primitive root; to eat.

If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Taste, judgment. From ta'am; properly, a taste, i.e. perception; by implication, intelligence; transitively, a mandate.
To flow (like slime). From ruwr; saliva; by resemblance, broth.
(a plant), probably a purslane. From chalam; probably purslain.
7: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To refuse. A primitive root; to refuse.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
Illness. From daveh; sickness; figuratively, loathing.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
8: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Request, thing asked for. Or shelah; from sha'al; a petition; by implication, a loan.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
9: God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
A primitive root; properly, to yield, especially assent; hence To undertake as an act of volition.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
A primitive root; to jump, i.e. Be violently agitated; causatively, to terrify, shake off, untie.
To cut off, break off, gain by violence. A primitive root to break off, i.e. plunder; figuratively, to finish, or stop.

To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To spring. A primitive root; probably to leap, i.e. Exult.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
A writhing, anguish. And chiylah; from chuwl; a throe.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
11: Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
To wait, await. A primitive root; to wait; by implication, to be patient, hope.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To be long. A primitive root; to be long.
12: Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.

A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!

Of bronze. Apparently passive participle of nachash (nachash, as denominative) when hissing); coppery, i.e. hard.

As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.

Or tushiyah; from an unused root probably meaning to substantiate; support or ability, i.e. help, an undertaking, understanding.
To impel, thrust, banish. A primitive root; to push off; used in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.

Kindness, piety, reproof, beauty. From chacad; kindness; by implication piety: rarely reproof, or Beauty.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
15: A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).

Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.

Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
16: To be dark. A primitive root; to be ashy, i.e. Dark-colored; by implication, to mourn.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Frost, ice. Or qorach; from qarach; ice; hence, hail; by resemblance, rock crystal.

To conceal. A primitive root; to veil from sight, i.e. Conceal.

Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To burn, scorch. A primitive root; to flow away.
To go out, be extinguished. A primitive root; to be extinguished; figuratively, to expire or be dried up.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
To be or become warm. A primitive root; to be hot.

A primitive root; properly, to bend, i.e. to clasp; also to turn around or aside.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
From an unused root meaning to lie waste; a desolation, i.e. Desert; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
19: A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
Or Temao; probably of foreign derivation; Tema, a son of Ishmael, and the region settled by him.
To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
A going, way, traveling company. Feminine of haliyk; a walking; by implication, a procession or march, a caravan.
Of foreign origin; Sheba, the name of three early progenitors of tribes and of an Ethiopian district.
To bind together, collect, to expect. A primitive root; to bind together, i.e. Collect; to expect.

To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
22: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To give. A primitive root; to give; generally, to put; imperatively come.
To give a present, bribe. A primitive root; to donate, i.e. Bribe.

Awe-inspiring, terror-striking. From arats; fearful, i.e. Powerful or tyrannical.

To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.

A primitive root; to stray, usually to mistake, especially to transgress; by extension to reel, be enraptured.

Desc. of Shuah. Patronymic from Shuwach; a Shuchite or descendant of Shuach.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.

Loins, stupidity, confidence. From kacal; properly, fatness, i.e. By implication the loin or the viscera; also silliness or trust.

A ship. Feminine of 'oniy; a ship.
An eagle. From an unused root meaning to lacerate; the eagle.
To rush, dart. A primitive root; to pounce as a bird of prey.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
27: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Complaint, musing. From siyach; a contemplation; by implication, an utterance.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To gleam, smile. A primitive root; to break off or loose, i.e. Desist or invade.
28: To be afraid, fear. A primitive root; to fear.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A hurt, injury, pain. From atsab; a idol; also, a pain or wound.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be empty or clean. A primitive root; to be clean; by implication to be bare, i.e. Extirpated.
29: I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
To be wicked, act wickedly. A primitive root; to be wrong; by implication, to disturb, violate.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To toil, grow or be weary. A primitive root; properly, to gasp; hence, to be exhausted, to tire, to toil.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
30: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To wash, wash off or away, bathe. A primitive root; to lave.
In. Prolongation for prepositional prefix; in, with, by, etc.
To be bright, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be transparent or clean.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
The same as bor; vegetable lye; used as a soap for washing, or a flux for metals.
31: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To dip. A primitive root; to dip, to immerse.
A pit. From shuwach; a pit; figuratively, destruction.
A wrapper, mantle. Transp. For simlah; a dress.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
32: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
33: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Two (a card. number). Dual of sheniy; feminine shttayim; two; also twofold.
34: To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Terror, dread. Or remah; from the same as 'ayom; fright; concrete, an idol.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To fall upon, startle, terrify. A primitive root; to fear.
35: To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.

Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.

Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.

God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.

From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Or shav; from the same as show' in the sense of desolating; evil, literally or morally; figuratively idolatry, uselessness.
Male, man. From the same as mathay; properly, an adult; by implication, a man.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.

Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.

Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.

Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Blemish, defect. Usually muwm; as if passive participle from an unused root probably meaning to stain; a blemish.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fear, to revere, caus, to frighten. A primitive root; to fear; morally, to revere; caus. To frighten.
16: To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
Trouble, labor, toil. From amal; toil, i.e. Wearing effort; hence, worry, wheth. Of body or mind.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.

A light, double light, noon. From tsahar; a light: dual double light, i.e. Noon.

Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.

To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
Security. From batach; properly, a place of refuge; abstract, safety, both the fact and the feeling; often safely.
19: A primitive root; to crouch; be implication, to recline, repose, brood, lurk, imbed.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To tremble, be terrified. A primitive root; to shudder with terror; hence, to fear; also to hasten.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
A primitive root; properly, to be rubbed or worn; hence to be weak, sick, afflicted; or to grieve, make sick; also to stroke, entreat.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
20: An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
Flight, place of escape or refuge. From nuwc; a retreat; abstractly, a fleeing.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
A breathing out. From naphach; a breathing out, i.e. Expiring.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.

1: A patriarch. From 'ayab; hated; Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
3: Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
4: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Laughter, derision, sport. Or schoq; from sachaq; laughter.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
Complete, sound. From tamam; entire; also integrity, truth.
Laughter, derision, sport. Or schoq; from sachaq; laughter.
5: At ease, secure. From sha'an; secure; in a bad sense, haughty.
Ruin, disaster. From an unused root probably meaning to pierce; misfortune.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
To slip, slide, totter, shake. A primitive root; to waver.
6: A primitive root; properly, to be burly, i.e. powerful; by implication, to ravage.
To be quiet or at ease. Or shalav; a primitive root; to be tranquil, i.e. Secure or successful.
To be agitated, quiver, quake, be excited, perturbed. A primitive root; to quiver.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Security, safety. Feminine plural from batach; security.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
7: But, but indeed. Apparently a variation of 'uwlay; however or on the contrary.
To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
8: Or. Presumed to be the constructive or genitival form of -av; short for 'avvah; desire; hence or, also if.
To muse, complain, talk (of). A primitive root; to ponder, i.e. converse or utter.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
A fish. Or dag; from dagah; a fish; or perhaps rather from da'ag; but still better from da'ag; a fish.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
9: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
10: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
11: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
To examine, try. A primitive root; to test; generally and figuratively, to investigate.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
Probably from chanak in the sense of tasting; properly, the palate or inside of the mouth; hence, the mouth itself.
To taste, perceive. A primitive root; to taste; figuratively, to perceive.
12: Aged. From yashesh; an old man.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
13: Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
14: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To throw down, break or tear down. A primitive root; to pull down or in pieces, break, &? Destroy.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To build. A primitive root; to build.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To shut, close. A primitive root; to shut up; figuratively, to surrender.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
15: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To restrain, retain. A primitive root; to inclose; by analogy, to hold back; also to maintain, rule, assemble.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To be dry, dried up, or withered. A primitive root; to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also to dry up or wither.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
16: Or tushiyah; from an unused root probably meaning to substantiate; support or ability, i.e. help, an undertaking, understanding.
Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To go astray, commit sin or error. A primitive root; to stray, i.e. sin.
A primitive root; to stray, usually to mistake, especially to transgress; by extension to reel, be enraptured.
17: To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Barefoot. Or sheylal; from shalal; nude; by implication, captive.
18: To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
To tie, bind, imprison. A primitive root; to yoke or hitch; by analogy, to fasten in any sense, to join battle.
A waist cloth. From 'aziqqiym; something girt; a belt, also a band.
Loins. From an unused root meaning to be slender; properly, the waist or small of the back; only in plural the loins.
19: Priest. Active participle of kahan; literally, one officiating, a priest; also an acting priest.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Barefoot. Or sheylal; from shalal; nude; by implication, captive.
To twist, pervert, overturn. A primitive root; properly, to wrench, i.e. to subvert.
Perennial, ever-flowing, permanence. Or ethan; from an unused root; permanence; hence permanent; specifically a chieftain.
20: To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Taste, judgment. From ta'am; properly, a taste, i.e. perception; by implication, intelligence; transitively, a mandate.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Inclined, generous, noble. From nadab; properly, voluntary, i.e. Generous; hence, magnanimous; as noun, a grandee.
A girdle. Or mezach; from zachach; a belt.
Sink, relax. A primitive root; to slacken.
A channel. From 'acaph; properly, containing, i.e. A tube; also a bed or valley of a stream; also a strong thing or a hero.

From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
23: Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
To grow, grow great.: a primitive root; to grow, i.e. to enlarge, laud.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.

To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.

To err. A primitive root; to vacillate, i.e. Reel or stray; also causative of both.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
From an unused root meaning to lie waste; a desolation, i.e. Desert; figuratively, a worthless thing; adverbially, in vain.

From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
