Stocks (for securing feet of prisoners). From an unused root meaning to estop; the stocks.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
To cut in, carve. A primitive root; to carve; by implication, to delineate; also to entrench.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A root. From sharash; a root.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
28: To become old, wear out. A primitive root; to fail; by implication to wear out, decay.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A moth. From ashesh; a moth.
A covering, clothing, treachery, pillage. From bagad; a covering, i.e. Clothing; also treachery or pillage.

1: Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Sated, satisfied, surfeited. From saba'; satiated.
Agitation, excitement, raging. From ragaz; commotion, restlessness, crash, disquiet, anger.
2: Or tsits; from tsuwts; properly, glistening, i.e. A burnished plate; also a flower; a wing.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Branch to be cut down off, circumcise. A primitive root; to become clipped or circumcised.
A shadow. From tsalal; shade, whether literal or figurative.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
3: To open, to be observant. A primitive root; to open; figuratively, to be observant.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
4: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Clean, pure. Or tahor; from taher; pure.
Unclean. From tame'; foul in a relig. Sense.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
5: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To cut, sharpen, decide. A primitive root; properly, to point sharply, i.e. to wound; figuratively, to be alert, to decide.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
6: A primitive root; to gaze at or about; by implication, to inspect, consider, compassionate, be nonplussed or bewildered.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To cease. A primitive root; properly, to be flabby, i.e. desist; be lacking or idle.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To be pleased with, accept favorably. A primitive root; to be pleased with; specifically, to satisfy a debt.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Hired. From sakar; a man at wages by the day or year.
7: A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
To pass on or away, pass through. A primitive root; properly, to slide by, i.e. to hasten away, pass on, spring up, pierce or change.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
A young shoot, twig. Feminine of yowneq; a sprout.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To cease. A primitive root; properly, to be flabby, i.e. desist; be lacking or idle.
8: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A root. From sharash; a root.
To be or become old. A primitive root; to be old.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A stock, stem. From an unused root meaning to cut down; the trunk or stump of a tree.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
9: To smell, perceive odor. From ruwach; odor.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
A primitive root; to break forth as a bud, i.e. Bloom; generally, to spread; specifically, to fly; figuratively, to flourish.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From qatsar; severed, i.e. Harvest, the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
Plantation, planting, plant. From nata'; a plant; collectively, a plantation; abstractly, a planting.
10: Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
To be weak or prostrate. A primitive root; to prostrate; by implication, to overthrow, decay.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To expire, perish, die. A primitive root; to breathe out, i.e. expire.
Where?. Perhaps from 'aiyn; where? Hence how?.
11: Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To go. A primitive root; to go away, hence, to disappear.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
To parch, to desolate, destroy, kill. Or chareb; a primitive root; to parch i.e. to desolate, destroy, kill.
To be dry, dried up, or withered. A primitive root; to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also to dry up or wither.
12: Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Constructive feminine of balah; properly, a failure of, i.e. not, except, without, unless, besides, because not, until, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Arise, be awake, watch. A primitive root (compare yaqats); to awake.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
Sleep. Or shena; from yashen; sleep.
13: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
14: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
To live, to revive. A primitive root; to live, whether literally or figuratively; causatively, to revive.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To wait, await. A primitive root; to wait; by implication, to be patient, hope.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
15: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
To long (for). A primitive root; properly, to become pale, i.e. to pine after; also to fear.
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
16: Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
A step, pace. From tsantarah; a pace or regular step.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Or chattacth; from chata'; an offence, and its penalty, occasion, sacrifice, or expiation; also an offender.
17: To seal, affix a seal, seal up. A primitive root; to close up; especially to seal.
A parcel, a kernel, particle. Or tsror; from tsarar; a parcel; also a kernel or particle.
To smear, plaster over, stick, glue. A primitive root; properly, to stick on as a patch; figuratively, to impute falsely.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
18: But, but indeed. Apparently a variation of 'uwlay; however or on the contrary.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To move, proceed, advance. A primitive root; to remove figuratively, to grow old; specifically, to transcribe.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
19: Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To rub away, beat fine, pulverize. A primitive root; to comminate.
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
From caphach; something falling off, i.e. A self-sown crop; figuratively, a freshet.
To overflow, rinse or wash off. A primitive root; to gush; by implication, to inundate, cleanse; by analogy, to gallop, conquer.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
20: To prevail over, overpower. A primitive root; to overpower.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To repeat, do again. A primitive root; to fold, i.e. Duplicate; by implication, to transmute.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
21: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To be or grow insignificant. A primitive root; to be small, i.e. ignoble.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
22: Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To be in pain. A primitive root; properly, to feel pain; by implication, to grieve; figuratively, to spoil.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To mourn. A primitive root; to bewail.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

1: From 'el and paz; God of gold; Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau.
Inhab. of Teman. Patronymically from Teyman; a Temanite or descendant of Teman.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
East, east wind. Or qadim; from qadam; the fore or front part; hence the East.
3: To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to be familiar with; by implication, to minister to, be serviceable to, be customary.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To confer or gain profit or benefit. A primitive root; properly, to ascend; figuratively, to be valuable.
4: Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
To diminish, restrain, withdraw. A primitive root; to scrape off; by implication, to shave, remove, lessen, withhold.
Complaint, musing. Feminine of siyach; reflection; be extension, devotion.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
5: Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
To learn. A primitive root, to associate with; hence, to learn.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
Crafty, shrewd, sensible. Passive participle of aram; cunning.
6: Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be wicked, act wickedly. A primitive root; to be wrong; by implication, to disturb, violate.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
7: Former, first, chief. Or riishon; from ri'shah; first, in place, time or rank.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Or chiyl; a primitive root; properly, to twist or whirl, i.e. to dance, to writhe in pain or fear; figuratively, to wait, to pervert.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A hill. Feminine from the same as Geba'; a hillock.
8: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Council, counsel. From yacad; a session, i.e. Company of persons; by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To diminish, restrain, withdraw. A primitive root; to scrape off; by implication, to shave, remove, lessen, withhold.
9: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
10: To be hoary. A primitive root; properly, to become aged, i.e. to grow gray.
Aged. From yashesh; an old man.
Great, mighty, much. From kabar; vast, whether in extent, or in number, many.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
11: Consolation. Or tanchum; and tanchuwmah; from nacham; compassion, solace.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Gentleness. From an unused root perhaps meaning to move softly; a necromancer, gently.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
12: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To wink, flash. A primitive root; to twinkle the eye: wink.
13: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
14: Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
To be clear, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be translucent; figuratively, to be innocent.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
15: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be bright, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be transparent or clean.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
16: Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
To be corrupt (morally). A primitive root; to muddle, i.e. to turn corrupt.
To drink. A primitive root; to imbibe.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
17: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To live, to declare, show. A primitive root; properly, to live; by implication to declare or show.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
18: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
19: Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Midst. From an unused root meaning to sever; a bisection, i.e. the centre.
20: Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Or chiyl; a primitive root; properly, to twist or whirl, i.e. to dance, to writhe in pain or fear; figuratively, to wait, to pervert.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
A year. (in plura or shanah; from shanah; a year.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Awe-inspiring, terror-striking. From arats; fearful, i.e. Powerful or tyrannical.
21: Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
A primitive root; properly, to be burly, i.e. powerful; by implication, to ravage.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
22: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
A primitive root; properly, to lean forward, i.e. To peer into the distance; by implication, to observe, await.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
23: A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
24: Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
Straits, stress. Or mtsuqah; feminine of matsuwq; narrowness, i.e. trouble.
To fall upon, startle, terrify. A primitive root; to fear.
To prevail over, overpower. A primitive root; to overpower.
Ready, prepared. From athad; prepared; by implication, skilful; feminine plural the future; also treasure.
Onset. Of uncertain derivation; perhaps tumult.
25: A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To be strong, mighty. A primitive root; to be strong; by implication, to prevail, act insolently.

If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To go down, descend. A primitive root; to sink, i.e. Descend; causatively, to press or lead down.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.

1: Perhaps Bel has loved, one of Job's friends. Of uncertain derivation; Bildad, one of Job's friends.
Desc. of Shuah. Patronymic from Shuwach; a Shuchite or descendant of Shuach.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: Where? whither?. Or manah; contracted from 'aiyn; where?; hence, whither?, when?; also hither and thither.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A snare, net. From an unused root probably meaning to wrench; perversion.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
3: Why? for what reason?. Or madduaa; from mah and the passive participle of yada'; what known?; i.e. why?.
A beast, animal, cattle. From an unused root; properly, a dumb beast; especially any large quadruped or animal.
To be stopped up, stupid. A collateral form of tame'; to be impure in a religious sense.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
4: To tear, rend, pluck. A primitive root; to pluck off or pull to pieces; causatively to supply with food.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To move, proceed, advance. A primitive root; to remove figuratively, to grow old; specifically, to transcribe.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
5: A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To go out, be extinguished. A primitive root; to be extinguished; figuratively, to expire or be dried up.
Probably a flame. From the same as shabab; flame.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To shine. A primitive root; to glitter; causatively, to illuminate.
6: A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To be or grow dark. A primitive root; to be dark; transitively, to darken.
A lamp. Or nir; also neyr; or ner; or nerah; from a primitive root properly, meaning to glisten; a lamp or light.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To go out, be extinguished. A primitive root; to be extinguished; figuratively, to expire or be dried up.
7: Vigor, wealth. Probably from the same as 'aven; ability, power, wealth.
A step, pace. From tsantarah; a pace or regular step.
A primitive root; to press, i.e. Be narrow; figuratively, be in distress.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
8: Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
A net. From yarash; a net.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Latticework, network. Feminine of sabak; a net-work, i.e a snare, a ballustrade; also a reticulated ornament to a pillar.
9: A, sheet, a spring net. From pachach; a sheet; also a spring net.
To grasp, take hold, take possession. A primitive root; to seize.
Heel, footprint, hind part. Or hiqqbah; from aqab; a heel; hence, a track; figuratively, the rear.
Perhaps a snare, trap. From the same as tsammah; a noose; figuratively, destruction.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
10: A rope, a measuring line, a district, inheritance, a noose, a company, a throe, ruin.
To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A catching instrument, a snare, trap. From lakad; a snare.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.
11: Terror, dreadful event, calamity, destruction. From balahh; alarm; hence, destruction.
To fall upon, startle, terrify. A primitive root; to fear.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
12: Vigor, wealth. Probably from the same as 'aven; ability, power, wealth.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Limping, stumbling. From tsala'; a limping or full.
13: To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A skin. From uwr; skin; by implication, hide, leather.
First-born. From bakar; firstborn; hence, chief.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
14: To pull, draw, or tear away, apart, or off. A primitive root; to tear off.
Confidence. From batach; properly, a refuge, i.e. security, or assurance.
To step, march. A primitive root; to pace, i.e. Step regularly; to mount; to march; to hurl.
Terror, dreadful event, calamity, destruction. From balahh; alarm; hence, destruction.
15: From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
A primitive root (by transmission) to shakab through the idea of lodging; compare cikluwth, shakam); to reside or permanently stay.
Brimstone. Probably feminine of gopher; properly, cypress- resin; by analogy, sulphur.
To scatter, fan, winnow. A primitive root; to toss about; by implication, to diffuse, winnow.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.
16: A root. From sharash; a root.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
To be dry, dried up, or withered. A primitive root; to be ashamed, confused or disappointed; also to dry up or wither.
From qatsar; severed, i.e. Harvest, the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb.
From alah; properly, the upper part, used only adverbially with prefix upward, above, overhead, from the top, etc.
Branch to be cut down off, circumcise. A primitive root; to become clipped or circumcised.
17: Remembrance, memorial. Or zeker; from zakar; a memento, abstr. Recollection; by implication, commemoration.
To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
The outside, a street. Or chuts; from an unused root meaning to sever; properly, separate by a wall, i.e. Outside, outdoors.
18: To thrust, drive, push. A prim root; to push away or down.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
From yabal; the earth; by extension, the globe; by implication, its inhabitants; specifically, a partic. Land, as Babylonia, Palestine.
19: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Progeny, posterity. From an unused root meaning to propagate; offspring.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
A survivor. From sarad; a survivor.
Or magur; from guwr in the sense of lodging; a temporary abode; by extension, a permanent residence.
20: Coming after or behind. Or;acharon; from 'achar; hinder; generally, late or last; specifically western.
To be desolated or appalled. A primitive root; to stun, i.e. Devastate or stupefy.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Former, eastern. Or qadmoniy; from qadmown; anterior or oriental.
To grasp, take hold, take possession. A primitive root; to seize.
A tempest, a terror. From sa'ar; a tempest; also a terror.
21: Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
Unjust, unrighteous one. Intensive from aval; evil.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

1: A patriarch. From 'ayab; hated; Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Where? whither?. Or manah; contracted from 'aiyn; where?; hence, whither?, when?; also hither and thither.
To suffer. A primitive root; to grieve.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
3: Ten. Masculine of term aasarah; from asar; ten.
A beat, foot, anvil, occurrence. Or pa;amah; from pa'am; a stroke, literally or figuratively.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To be humiliated. A primitive root; properly, to wound; but only figuratively, to taunt or insult.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
Perhaps to wrong. A primitive root; apparently to injure.
4: Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
A primitive root; to stray, usually to mistake, especially to transgress; by extension to reel, be enraptured.
Error. From an unused root meaning to stray; mistake.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
With (denoting proximity). Probably from 'anah; properly, nearness, near; hence, generally, with, by, at, among, etc.
5: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A reproach. From charaph. contumely, disgrace, the pudenda.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
6: Then. Or hephowe; from poh; strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To be bent or crooked. A primitive root; to wrest.
A net, a, fastness, tower. Or mtsowdah; or mtsodah; from tsuwd; a net.
A primitive root; to strike with more or less violence; by implication to knock together, i.e. Surround or circulate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
7: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; to proclaim.
Violence, wrong. From chamac; violence; by implication, wrong; by meton. Unjust gain.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
8: To wall up or off, to build a wall. A primitive root; to wall in or around.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
9: To strip off, make a dash, raid. A primitive root; to spread out; by analogy, to strip.
Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
A crown, wreath. From atar; a crown.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
10: To pull down, break down. A primitive root; to tear down.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To pull out or up, set out, journey. A primitive root; properly, to pull up, especially the tent-pins, i.e. Start on a journey.
A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
11: A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To burn or be kindled with anger. A primitive root; to glow or grow warm; figuratively to blaze up, of anger, zeal, jealousy.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
12: A band, troop. From gadad; a crowd.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
To lift up, cast up. A primitive root; to mound up; figurative, to exalt; reflexively, to oppose.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; properly, to incline; by implication, to decline; specifically, to pitch a tent; gen. To encamp.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
13: To be or become far or distant. A primitive root; to widen, i.e. recede or remove.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
14: Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.
To cease. A primitive root; properly, to be flabby, i.e. desist; be lacking or idle.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To forget. Or shakeach; a primitive root; to mislay, i.e. To be oblivious of, from want of memory or attention.
15: A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
A primitive root; properly, to turn aside from the road, i.e. Sojourn; also to shrink, fear; also to gather for hostility.
A maid, handmaid. Apparently a primitive word; a maid-servant or female slave.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Foreign, alien. From neker; strange, in a variety of degrees and applications.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
16: To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
In. Prolongation for prepositional prefix; in, with, by, etc.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
17: Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
18: A young boy. From uwl; a babe.
To spurn, to disappear. A primitive root; to spurn; also to disappear.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
19: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Male, man. From the same as mathay; properly, an adult; by implication, a man.
Council, counsel. From yacad; a session, i.e. Company of persons; by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret.
To abhor. A primitive root; to loathe, i.e. detest.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
20: A skin. From uwr; skin; by implication, hide, leather.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
A skin. From uwr; skin; by implication, hide, leather.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
21: A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
Friend, companion, fellow. Or reya2; from ra'ah; an associate.
A primitive root; properly, to bend or stoop in kindness to an inferior; to favor, bestow; causatively to implore.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
22: To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
23: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Then. Or hephowe; from poh; strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
To write. A primitive root; to grave, by implication, to write.
To cut in, inscribe, decree. A primitive root; properly, to hack, i.e. Engrave; by implication, to enact or Prescribe.
A missive, document, writing, book. Or ciphrah; from caphar; properly, writing; by implication, a book.
24: Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
A stylus. From iyt in the sense of swooping, i.e. Side-long stroke; a stylus or marking stick.
Lead (a metal). Or mophereth; feminine participle active of aphar; lead.
Or chatseb; a primitive root; to cut or carve, stone or other material); by implication, to hew, split, square, quarry, engrave.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
25: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
To redeem, act as kinsman. A primitive root, to redeem, i.e. To be the next of kin.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Coming after or behind. Or;acharon; from 'achar; hinder; generally, late or last; specifically western.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
26: The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
A skin. From uwr; skin; by implication, hide, leather.
A primitive root; to strike with more or less violence; by implication to knock together, i.e. Surround or circulate.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
27: See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to turn aside; hence to be a foreigner, strange, profane; specifically to commit adultery.
A kidney. Feminine of kliy; a kidney; figuratively, the mind.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
Bosom. Or cheq; and chowq; from an unused root, apparently meaning to inclose; the bosom.
28: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
A root. From sharash; a root.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
29: A primitive root; properly, to turn aside from the road, i.e. Sojourn; also to shrink, fear; also to gather for hostility.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Judgment. From diyn; judgement; by impl. Also strife.

1: One of Job's friends. From tsaphar; departing; Tsophar, a friend of Job.
Inhab. of Naamah. Patrial from a place corresponding in name with Na'amah; a Naamathite, or inhabitant of Naamah.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Divided, a sentiment. Or samiph; from ca'aph; divided, i.e. a sentiment.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Or rabur; passive participle of abar; properly, crossed, i.e. transit; used only adverbially, on account of, in order that.
A primitive root; to hurry; figuratively, to be eager with excitement or enjoyment.
3: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
Discipline, chastening, correction. From yacar; properly, chastisement; figuratively, reproof, warning or instruction; also restraint.
Insult, reproach, ignominy. From kalam; disgrace.
4: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Perpetuity. From adah; properly, a terminus, i.e. duration, in the sense of advance or perpetuity.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Near. Or qarob; from qarab; near.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Profane, irreligious. From chaneph; soiled, impious.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
A moment. From raga'. A wink, i.e. A very short space of time.
6: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Loftiness. From the same as sow' by permutation; elevation.
A primitive root; to ascend, intransitively or actively; used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
7: To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Dung. A variation of galal; dung.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Where?. Perhaps from 'aiyn; where? Hence how?.
8: To fly, to faint. A primitive root; to cover; hence to fly; also to faint.
A dream. Or chalom; from chalam; a dream.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
Vision. From chazah; a revelation, expectation by dream.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
9: An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To catch sight of, look on. A primitive root; to tan; figuratively to scan.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
To behold, regard. A primitive root; to spy out, i.e. survey, lurk for, care for.
10: Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To be pleased with, accept favorably. A primitive root; to be pleased with; specifically, to satisfy a debt.
Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Vigor, wealth. Probably from the same as 'aven; ability, power, wealth.
11: Youth, youthful vigor. Passive participle of alam in the denominative sense of elem; adolescence; figuratively, vigor.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
12: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To become or be sweet or pleasant. A primitive root; to suck, by implication, to relish, or be sweet.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
13: To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
To withhold, hold back. A primitive root; to debar from benefit or injury.
Midst. From an unused root meaning to sever; a bisection, i.e. the centre.
Probably from chanak in the sense of tasting; properly, the palate or inside of the mouth; hence, the mouth itself.
14: Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
Or mrowrah; from marar; properly, bitterness; concretely, a bitter thing; specifically bile; also venom.
(a venomous serpent) perhaps cobra. From an unused root meaning to twist; an asp.
Inward part, midst. From qarab; properly, the nearest part, i.e. The center, whether literal, figurative or adverbial.
15: To swallow down, swallow up, engulf. A primitive root; to make away with; generally, to destroy.
From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
Spue out, vomit out, up, up again. Or qayah; a primitive root; to vomit.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Or yaresh; a primitive root; to occupy; by implication, to seize, to rob, to inherit; also to expel, to impoverish, to ruin.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
16: To suck. A primitive root; to suck; causatively, to give milk.
(bitter and poisonous herb) venom. Or rowsh; apparently the same as ro'sh; a poisonous plant, probably the poppy; generally poison.
(a venomous serpent) perhaps cobra. From an unused root meaning to twist; an asp.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
(a kind of) viper. From epha'; an asp or other venomous serpent.
To kill, slay. A primitive root; to smite with deadly intent.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A stream, division. From palag; a runlet, i.e. Gully.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
Honey. From an unused root meaning to be gummy; honey; by analogy, syrup.
Curd. Or chemah; from the same root as chowmah; curdled milk or cheese.

From chuwl; probably a force, whether of men, means or other resources; an army, wealth, virtue, valor, strength.
Exchange, recompense. From muwr; barter, compensation.
19: To crush. A primitive root; to crack in pieces, literally or figuratively.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
Dangling, weak, thin. From dalal; properly, dangling, i.e. weak or thin.
To tear away, seize, rob. A primitive root; to pluck off; specifically to flay, strip or rob.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To build. A primitive root; to build.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.

A survivor. From sarad; a survivor.

So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.

A primitive root; to press, i.e. Be narrow; figuratively, be in distress.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

(burning of) anger. Or charon; from charah; a burning of anger.

Probably misfortune. From a primitive root meaning to strike: a crushing; figuratively, calamity.
