Heat, rage. Or chemac; from yacham; heat; figuratively, anger, poison.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
21: Delight, pleasure. From chaphets; pleasure; hence desire; concretely, a valuable thing; hence a matter.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
A primitive root; properly, to chop into, pierce or sever; hence, to curtail, to distribute; as denom. From chets, to shoot an arrow.
22: A primitive root; properly, to goad, i.e. to teach:(un-) accustomed, X diligently, expert, instruct, learn, skilful, teach(-er,ing).
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
23: This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
From tamam; completeness; figuratively, prosperity; usually innocence.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Being at ease. For sha'anan; tranquil.
Quiet, at ease. Or shaleyv; feminine shlevah; from shalah; tranquil; careless; abstractly, security.
24: Probably a pail, bucket. From an unused root meaning apparently to contain; a receptacle.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Milk. From the same as cheleb; milk.
Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
Cause to drink water, give to drink. A primitive root; to quaff, i.e. to irrigate or furnish a potion to.
Marrow. From the same as meach; fat, i.e. Marrow.
25: This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
To die. A primitive root: to die; causatively, to kill.
Bitter, bitterness. Or marah; from marar; bitter; also bitterness, or bitterly.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
26: Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
A worm. From ramam in the sense of breading; a maggot, literally or figuratively.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
27: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Thought, device. Or machashebeth; from chashab; a contrivance, i.e. a texture, machine, or intention, plan.
Purpose, discretion, device. From zamam; a plan, usually evil, sometimes good.
To treat violently or wrong. A primitive root; to be violent; by implication, to maltreat.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
28: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Inclined, generous, noble. From nadab; properly, voluntary, i.e. Generous; hence, magnanimous; as noun, a grandee.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Dwelling place, tabernacle. From shakan; a residence; specifically, the Tabernacle.
29: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
To pass over, through, or by, pass on. A primitive root; to cross over; used very widely of any transition; specifically, to cover.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A sign. Probably from 'uwth; a signal, as a flag, beacon, monument, omen, prodigy, evidence, etc.
30: Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
To withhold, refrain. A primitive root; to restrain or Refrain; by implication, to refuse, spare, preserve; also to observe.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Distress, calamity. From the same as 'uwd; oppression; by implication misfortune, ruin.
To conduct, bear along. A primitive root; properly, to flow; causatively, to bring.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
31: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
32: To conduct, bear along. A primitive root; properly, to flow; causatively, to bring.
A grave, sepulcher. Or qibrah; from qabar; a sepulchre.
To watch, wake. A primitive root; to be alert, i.e. Sleepless; hence to be on the lookout.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A stack of sheaves, a tomb. From an unused root; a stack of sheaves; by analogy, a tomb.
33: A clod (of earth). From an unused root meaning to pile together; a lump of clay.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
To become or be sweet or pleasant. A primitive root; to suck, by implication, to relish, or be sweet.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
To draw, drag. A primitive root; to draw, used in a great variety of applications.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.
34: How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
A return, answer. Or tshubah; from shuwb; a recurrence; a reply.
To remain, be left over. A primitive root; properly, to swell up, i.e. Be redundant.
An unfaithful or treacherous act. From ma'al; treachery, i.e. Sin.

1: From 'el and paz; God of gold; Eliphaz, the name of one of Job's friends, and of a son of Esau.
Inhab. of Teman. Patronymically from Teyman; a Temanite or descendant of Teman.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
A primitive root; to be familiar with; by implication, to minister to, be serviceable to, be customary.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
A primitive root; to be familiar with; by implication, to minister to, be serviceable to, be customary.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
3: Delight, pleasure. From chaphets; pleasure; hence desire; concretely, a valuable thing; hence a matter.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Gain made by violence, unjust gain, profit. From batsa'; plunder; by extension, gain.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A primitive root; to complete, in a good or a bad sense, literal, or figurative, transitive or intransitive.
4: A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
5: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
Iniquity, guilt, punishment for iniquity. Or oavown; from avah; perversity, i.e. evil.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
6: Out of favor. From chen; gratis, i.e. Devoid of cost, reason or advantage.
A primitive root; to wind tightly, i.e. To bind; specifically, by a pledge; figuratively, to pervert, destroy; also to writhe in pain.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
To strip off, make a dash, raid. A primitive root; to spread out; by analogy, to strip.
Naked. Or marom; from aram; nude, either partially or totally.
A covering, clothing, treachery, pillage. From bagad; a covering, i.e. Clothing; also treachery or pillage.
7: Cause to drink water, give to drink. A primitive root; to quaff, i.e. to irrigate or furnish a potion to.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To withhold, hold back. A primitive root; to debar from benefit or injury.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
8: Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
9: A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Emptily, vainly. From reyq; emptily; figuratively ineffectually, undeservedly.
Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.
An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
To crush. A primitive root; to crumble; transitively, to bruise.
10: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
A, sheet, a spring net. From pachach; a sheet; also a spring net.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
Suddenness, suddenly. Or pith.om; from petha'; instantly.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
11: Or. Presumed to be the constructive or genitival form of -av; short for 'avvah; desire; hence or, also if.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Abundance, quantity. Feminine of shepha'; copiousness.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
12: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
Height. From gabahh; elation, grandeur, arrogance.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A star. Probably from the same as Kabbown or kavah; a star; figuratively, a prince.
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
13: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
From ad with prepositional prefix; in up to or over against; generally at, beside, among, behind, for, etc.
Cloud, heavy cloud. Probably from araph; gloom.
14: A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Vault, horizon. From chuwg; a circle.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
15: To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
Male, man. From the same as mathay; properly, an adult; by implication, a man.
To tread, march. A primitive root; to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string a bow.
16: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To seize. A primitive root; to pluck, i.e. Destroy.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
Foundation, base. From yacad; a foundation.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
17: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
Do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, especially to practise.
18: To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
To be or become far or distant. A primitive root; to widen, i.e. recede or remove.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
19: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
To mock, deride, stammer. A primitive root; to deride; by implication to speak unintelligibly.
20: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Adversary. From quwm; an opponent, i.e. enemies.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
From yathar; properly, an overhanging, i.e. an excess, superiority, remainder; also a small rope.
21: A primitive root; to be familiar with; by implication, to minister to, be serviceable to, be customary.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
22: To take. A primitive root; to take.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
23: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To build. A primitive root; to build.
To be or become far or distant. A primitive root; to widen, i.e. recede or remove.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
24: To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Precious ore, an ingot. From batsar; strictly a clipping, i.e. Gold.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Or pophiyr; and fowphir; of uncertain derivation; Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
25: The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Precious ore, an ingot. From batsar; strictly a clipping, i.e. Gold.
Eminence. From ya'aph; weariness, i.e. toil or speed.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
26: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To be soft, delicate, dainty. A primitive root; to be soft or pliable, i.e. effeminate or luxurious.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
27: To pray, supplicate. A primitive root; to burn incense in worship, i.e. Intercede.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
A vow. Or neder; from nadar; a promise; also a thing promised.
28: To cut, divide. A primitive root; to cut down or off; to destroy, divide, exclude, or decide.
Promise, speech, thing, word. The same as 'emer.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To shine. A primitive root; to glitter; causatively, to illuminate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
29: To be or become low, to be abased. A primitive root; to depress or sink.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Pride. The same as gevah; exaltation; arrogance.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
Low, lowly. From shachach; sunk, i.e. Downcast.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
30: A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.

2: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Complaint, musing. From siyach; a contemplation; by implication, an utterance.
Rebellion. From marah; bitterness, i.e. rebellion; concretely, bitter, or rebellious.
Or kabed; a primitive root; to be heavy, i.e. In a bad sense or in a good sense (in the same two senses).

To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.

Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.

A word, speech, utterance. From malal
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Surely, howbeit. Akin to 'aken; a particle of affirmation, surely; hence only.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
7: There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To escape. A primitive root; to slip out, i.e. Escape; causatively, to deliver.
8: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
The front, of place, time. Or qedmah; from qadam; the front, of place or time; often used adverbially.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
The hind side, back part. Or achor; from Achiyra'; the hinder part; hence behind, backward; also the West.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

9: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
To shroud, clothe, to languish. A primitive root; to shroud, i.e. Clothe; hence to languish.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
10: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To examine, try. A primitive root; to test; generally and figuratively, to investigate.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.
11: Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
To grasp, take hold, take possession. A primitive root; to seize.
Step, going. Or ashshur; from 'ashar in the sense of going; a step.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
12: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To depart, remove. A primitive root; to withdraw.
Commandment. From tsavah; a command, whether human or divine.
Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
13: One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To incline, desire. A primitive root; to wish for.
14: A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
15: Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
To dread, be in dread or in awe. A primitive root; to be startled; hence, to fear in general.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
16: God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To be tender, weak or soft. A primitive root; to soften, used figuratively.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To disturb, terrify. A primitive root; to tremble inwardly, i.e. be alarmed or agitated; by implication to hasten anxiously.
17: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To put an end to, exterminate. A primitive root; to extirpate.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
Darkness, gloom. From the same as 'aphel; dusk.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.

1: Why? for what reason?. Or madduaa; from mah and the passive participle of yada'; what known?; i.e. why?.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to hide; by implication, to hoard or reserve; figuratively to deny; specifically to protect, to lurk.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
Why may those who know Him
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
2: To reach, overtake. A primitive root; to reach.
Border, boundary. Or gbulah; feminine of gbuwl; a boundary, region.
A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
To tear away, seize, rob. A primitive root; to pluck off; specifically to flay, strip or rob.
A flock, herd. From adar; an arrangement, i.e. Muster.
3: A primitive root; to drive forth, i.e. Lead, carry away; reflexively, to proceed; also, to sigh.
A male ass. Or chamor; from chamar; a male ass.
An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
A head of cattle (bullock, ox, etc.). From shuwr; a bullock.
A primitive root; to wind tightly, i.e. To bind; specifically, by a pledge; figuratively, to pervert, destroy; also to writhe in pain.
4: A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Poor, afflicted, humble, meek. Or Aanayv; from anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To withdraw, hide. A primitive root; to secrete.
5: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
A wild donkey. Or pereh; from para' in the secondary sense of running wild; the onager.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Doing, deed, work. From pa'al; an act or work.
To look early or diligently for. A primitive root; properly, to dawn, i.e. be early at any task; by extension, to search for.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
From arab; a desert; especially the sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
A boy, lad, youth, retainer. From na'ar; a boy, from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also, a girl.
6: To dock off, curtail, to harvest. A primitive root; to dock off, i.e. Curtail; especially to harvest.
Fodder. From balal; mixed, i.e. feed.
Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.
To take the second crop, to take everything. A primitive root; to gather the after crop.
A vineyard. From an unused root of uncertain meaning; a garden or vineyard.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
7: From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
A garment, clothing, raiment. Or lbush; from labash; a garment; by implication A wife.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
Naked. Or marom; from aram; nude, either partially or totally.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
A covering. From kacah; a cover; figuratively, a veiling.
8: To be moist. A primitive root; to be moist.
Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
A flood of rain, rainstorm, downpour. From zaram; a gush of water.
To clasp, embrace. A primitive root; to clasp.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
Refuge, shelter. Or machceh; from chacah; a shelter.
9: An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
To tear away, seize, rob. A primitive root; to pluck off; specifically to flay, strip or rob.
The breast of a, woman, animal. Or shod; probably from shuwd contracted; the breast of a woman or animal.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
A primitive root; to wind tightly, i.e. To bind; specifically, by a pledge; figuratively, to pervert, destroy; also to writhe in pain.
10: From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
A garment, clothing, raiment. Or lbush; from labash; a garment; by implication A wife.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Naked. Or marom; from aram; nude, either partially or totally.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
A heap, a sheaf, an omer (dry measure). From amar; properly, a heap, i.e. A sheaf; also an omer, as a dry measure.
11: To press out oil. A primitive root; to glisten; used only as denominative from yitshar, to press out oil.
A wall. From shuwr; a wall.
To tread, march. A primitive root; to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string a bow.
Wine vat. From an unused root meaning to excavate; a trough; specifically, a wine-vat.
To be thirsty. A primitive root; to thirst.
12: Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Male, man. From the same as mathay; properly, an adult; by implication, a man.
To groan. A primitive root; to groan.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Pierced, polluted. From chalal; pierced; figuratively, polluted.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Unsavoriness, unseemliness. From the same as taphel; frivolity.
13: They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To rebel. A primitive root; to rebel.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.
14: A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To murder, slay. A primitive root; properly, to dash in pieces, i.e. Kill, especially to murder.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To slay. A primitive root; properly, to cut off, i.e. put to death.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A thief. From ganab; a stealer.
15: An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To commit adultery. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to apostatize.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
Twilight. From nashaph; properly, a breeze, i.e. dusk.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To behold, regard. A primitive root; to spy out, i.e. survey, lurk for, care for.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
16: From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
To dig, row. A primitive root; to force a passage, as by burglary; figuratively, with oars.
A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
Daytime, by day. From yowm; daily.
To seal, affix a seal, seal up. A primitive root; to close up; especially to seal.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
17: Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Morn- ing. From baqar; properly, dawn; generally, morning.
Terror, dreadful event, calamity, destruction. From balahh; alarm; hence, destruction.
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
18: Light, swift, fleet. Contracted from qalal; light; rapid.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Smoothness, flattery, an allotment. Feminine of cheleq; properly, smoothness; figuratively, flattery; also an allotment.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To be slight, swift or trifling. A primitive root; to be light, literally Or figuratively.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To turn. A primitive root; to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. Appear, look, etc.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A vineyard. From an unused root of uncertain meaning; a garden or vineyard.
19: Dryness, drought. From an unused root meaning to parch; aridity; concretely, a desert.
To tear away, seize, rob. A primitive root; to pluck off; specifically to flay, strip or rob.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Or shol; from sha'al; Hades or the world of the dead, including its accessories and inmates.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.

A worm. From ramam in the sense of breading; a maggot, literally or figuratively.
To become or be sweet or pleasant. A primitive root; to suck, by implication, to relish, or be sweet.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
21: A primitive root; to tend a flock; i.e. Pasture it; intransitively, to graze; generally to rule; by extension, to associate with.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
22: Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
To draw, drag. A primitive root; to draw, used in a great variety of applications.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
23: To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Security. From batach; properly, a place of refuge; abstract, safety, both the fact and the feeling; often safely.
To lean, support oneself. A primitive root; to support one's self.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
24: To be exalted. A primitive root; to rise.
A little, fewness, a few. Or miat; from ma'at; a little or few.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To be low or humiliated. A primitive root; to tumble; figuratively, to perish.
Draw together, shut. A primitive root; to draw together, i.e. Close; by implication, to leap; specifically, to die.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Branch to be cut down off, circumcise. A primitive root; to become clipped or circumcised.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
Ear (of grain). Or shibboleth; from the same as shebel; a stream; also an ear of grain; by analogy, a branch.
25: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Then. Or hephowe; from poh; strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To lie, be a liar. A primitive root; to lie, literally or figuratively.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.

Desc. of Shuah. Patronymic from Shuwach; a Shuchite or descendant of Shuach.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: To rule, have dominion, reign. A primitive root; to rule.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
Height. From ruwm; altitude, i.e. Concretely, abstractly (elation), or adverbially (aloft).
3: A band, troop. From gadad; a crowd.
Number, tally. From caphar; a number, definite or indefinite; also narration.

God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.

As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Moon. From the same as yerach; the moon.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be clear, shine. A primitive root; to be clear.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be bright, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be transparent or clean.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

Man, mankind. From 'anash; properly, a mortal; hence, a man in general.
A worm. From ramam in the sense of breading; a maggot, literally or figuratively.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.

Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
9: To grasp, take hold, take possession. A primitive root; to seize.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Seat of honor, throne. Or kicceh; from kacah; properly, covered, i.e. A throne.
To spread. A root apparently formed by compounding parash and that of paraz; to expand.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A cloud mass, cloud. From anan; a cloud, i.e. The nimbus or thunder-cloud.
10: To draw around, make a circle. A primitive root; to describe a circle.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
End, completeness. From kalah; completion; by implication, an extremity.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
11: A pillar, column. Or ammud; from amad; a column; also a stand, i.e. Platform.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
To triturate, to agitate. A primitive root; properly, to triturate, i.e. to agitate.
To be astounded or dumbfounded. A primitive root; to be in consternation.
A rebuke. From ga'ar; a chiding.
12: Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
A primitive root; properly, to toss violently and suddenly; figuratively to settle, i.e. Quiet; specifically, to wink.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
An understanding. And tbuwnah; or towbunah; from biyn; intelligence; by implication, an argument; by extension, caprice.
A primitive root; to dash asunder; by implication, to crush, smash or violently plunge; figuratively, to subdue or destroy.
Storm, a name for Eg. The same as rahab; Rahab, an epithet of Egypt.
13: Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
Fairness, clearness (of sky). From shaphar; brightness.
A primitive root; properly, to bore, i.e. to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane, to break, to begin; denom. to play.
Fleeing. Or bariach; from barach; a fugitive, i.e. The serpent, and the constellation by that name.
A serpent. From nachash; a snake.
14: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
An end. Feminine of qatseh; a termination.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A whisper. From an unused root meaning to emit a sound; an inkling.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Thunder. From ra'am; a peal of thunder.
Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.

1: A patriarch. From 'ayab; hated; Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To be bitter. A primitive root; properly, to trickle; but used only as a denominative from mar; to be bitter.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
3: Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
Breath. From nasham; a puff, i.e. Wind, angry or vital breath, divine inspiration, intellect. Or an animal.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
4: Or sepheth; probably from caphah or shaphah through the idea of termination; the lip; by implication, language; by analogy, a margin.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Injustice, unrighteousness. Or lavel; and lavlah; or owlah; or.olah; from aval; evil.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To moan, growl, utter, speak, muse. A primitive root; to murmur; by implication, to ponder.
Remissness, treachery. From ramah; remissness, treachery.
5: Far be it! Or chalilah; a directive from chalal; literal for a profaned thing; used Far be it!
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
To expire, perish, die. A primitive root; to breathe out, i.e. expire.
6: A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Sink, relax. A primitive root; to slacken.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To pull off, to expose, to betroth, to carp at, defame, to spend the winter.
7: Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
Unjust, unrighteous one. Intensive from aval; evil.
8: A cord, expectancy. From qavah; literally, a cord (compare qaveh); figuratively, expectancy.
Profane, irreligious. From chaneph; soiled, impious.
To cut off, break off, gain by violence. A primitive root to break off, i.e. plunder; figuratively, to finish, or stop.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
9: God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A cry, outcry. From tsa'aq; a shriek.
Tightness, a female rival. Feminine of tsar; tightness; transitively, a female rival.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
10: To be soft, delicate, dainty. A primitive root; to be soft or pliable, i.e. effeminate or luxurious.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
11: Or yara; a primitive root; properly, to flow as water; transitively, to lay or throw; figuratively, to point out, to teach.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To hide. A primitive root; to secrete, by act or word; hence to destroy.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
12: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Or Habel; from habal; emptiness or vanity; figuratively, something transitory and unsatisfactory; often used as an adverb.
To act emptily, become vain. A primitive root; to be vain in act, word, or expectation; specifically to lead astray.
13: This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Portion, tract, territory. From chalaq; properly, smoothness; also an allotment.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
From nachal; properly, something inherited, i.e. occupancy, or an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion.
Awe-inspiring, terror-striking. From arats; fearful, i.e. Powerful or tyrannical.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
14: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
To or foreign. A prol. And separable form of the prepositional prefix; to or for.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Issue, offspring, produce. From yatsa'; issue, i.e. Produce, children.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
15: A survivor. From sarad; a survivor.
To bury. A primitive root; to intern.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To weep, bewail. A primitive root; to weep; generally to bemoan.
16: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To heap up. A primitive root; to aggregate.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Raiment, attire. Or malbush; from labash; a garment, or clothing.
From chamar; properly, a bubbling up, i.e. Of water, a wave; of earth, mire or clay; also a heap; hence, a chomer or dry measure.
17: A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
To be smooth, to apportion, separate. A primitive root; to be smooth; by implication to apportion or separate.
Clean, free from, exempt. Or naqiyi,; from naqah; innocent.
18: A house. Probably from banah abbreviated; a house.
To build. A primitive root; to build.
A moth. From ashesh; a moth.
A thicket, booth. Fem of cok; a hut or lair.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
To watch, guard, keep. A primitive root; to guard, in a good sense Or a bad one.
19: To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
Rich. From ashar; rich, whether literal or figurative.
but will do so no more;
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.
To open, to be observant. A primitive root; to open; figuratively, to be observant.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
20: Terror, dreadful event, calamity, destruction. From balahh; alarm; hence, destruction.
To reach, overtake. A primitive root; to reach.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
A storm wind. From cuwph; a hurricane.
To steal. A primitive root; to thieve; by implication, to deceive.
Night. Or leyl; also laylah; from the same as luwl; properly, a twist, i.e. Night; figuratively, adversity.
21: East, east wind. Or qadim; from qadam; the fore or front part; hence the East.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.

To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.

Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To hiss, whistle, pipe. A primitive root; properly, to be shrill, i.e. To whistle or hiss.

Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.

Gold. From an unused root meaning to shimmer; gold, figuratively, something gold-colored, as oil, a clear sky.

Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
Copper, bronze. Or nchushah; feminine of nachuwsh; copper.

End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.

Darkness, gloom. From the same as 'aphel; dusk.

Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
