19: (a precious stone) perhaps topaz. Of foreign derivation; a gem, probably the topaz.
Probably of foreign origin; Cush, the name of a son of Ham, and of his territory; also of an Israelite.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To set in a, row, arrange, put in order. A primitive root; to set in a row, i.e. Arrange, put in order.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To make light of, toss aside. A primitive root; to hang up, i.e. Weigh, or contemn.
Clean, pure. Or tahor; from taher; pure.
Gold. From katham; properly, something carved out, i.e. Ore; hence, gold.
20: Whence?. Probably identical with 'ayin in the sense of query.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Where?. Perhaps from 'aiyn; where? Hence how?.
This, here. A primitive word; the masculine demonstrative pronoun, this or that.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
21: To conceal. A primitive root; to veil from sight, i.e. Conceal.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide, literally or figuratively.
Flying creatures. From uwph; a bird, often collectively.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
22: (place of) destruction or ruin, Abaddon. Intensive from 'abad; abstract, a perishing; concrete, Hades.
Death. From muwth; death; concretely, the dead, their place or state; figuratively, pestilence, ruin.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
A hearing, report. From shama'; something heard, i.e. A sound, rumor, announcement; abstractly, audience.
An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
23: Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Or maqom; also mqowmah; or mqomah; from quwm; properly, a standing, i.e. A spot; but used widely of a locality; also of a condition.
24: To look. A primitive root; to scan, i.e. Look intently at; by implication, to regard with pleasure, favor or care.
An end. Feminine of qatseh; a termination.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
25: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Weight. From shaqal; weight; hence, weighing.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
A primitive root; to balance, i.e. Measure out; figuratively, arrange, equalize, through the idea of levelling.
Feminine of mad; properly, extension, i.e. Height or breadth; also a measure; hence a portion or a vestment; specifically, tribute.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
26: To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Something prescribed or owed, a statute. From chaqaq; an enactment; hence, an appointment.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
A thunderbolt, lightning flash. From an unused root meaning to glare; a flash of lightning.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
27: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
To search. A primitive root; properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately.
28: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
A fear. Feminine of yare'; fear; morally, reverence.
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Bad, evil. From ra'a'; bad or evil.

1: A patriarch. From 'ayab; hated; Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
2: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Month. From a unused root of uncertain signification; a lunation, i.e. Month.
The front, of place, time. Or qedmah; from qadam; the front, of place or time; often used adverbially.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
To keep, watch, preserve. A primitive root; properly, to hedge about, i.e. Guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
3: A lamp. Or nir; also neyr; or ner; or nerah; from a primitive root properly, meaning to glisten; a lamp or light.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
4: Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Harvest time, autumn. From charaph. properly, the crop gathered, i.e. the autumn season; figuratively, ripeness of age.
Council, counsel. From yacad; a session, i.e. Company of persons; by implication, intimacy, consultation, a secret.
God, god. Probably prolonged From 'el; a deity or the Deity.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
5: The Almighty. From shadad; the Almighty.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
A boy, lad, youth, retainer. From na'ar; a boy, from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also, a girl.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
6: A step. From halak; a walk, i.e. a step.
To wash, wash off or away, bathe. A primitive root; to lave.
Curd. Or chemah; from the same root as chowmah; curdled milk or cheese.
Rock, cliff. Or tsur; from tsuwr; properly, a cliff; generally, a rock or boulder; figuratively, a refuge; also an edge.
To pour out, melt. A primitive root (of orifice); to pour out, i.e. smelt, utter.
A channel, canal. From palag; a rill.
Fat, oil. From shaman; grease, especially liquid; figuratively, richness.
7: A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Town, city. From qarah in the sense of building; a city.
A gate. From sha'ar in its original sense; an opening, i.e. Door or gate.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Or moshab; from yashab; a seat; figuratively, a site; abstractly, a session; by extension an abode; by implication, population.
A broad open place, plaza. Or rchowb; from rachab; a width, i.e. avenue or area.
8: A boy, lad, youth, retainer. From na'ar; a boy, from the age of infancy to adolescence; by implication, a servant; also, a girl.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
To withdraw, hide. A primitive root; to secrete.
Aged. From yashesh; an old man.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
9: Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
To restrain, retain. A primitive root; to inclose; by analogy, to hold back; also to maintain, rule, assemble.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
10: Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
A leader, ruler, prince. Or nagid; from nagad; a commander, civil, military or religious; generally, honorable themes.
To withdraw, hide. A primitive root; to secrete.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
to the roofs of their mouths.
Probably from chanak in the sense of tasting; properly, the palate or inside of the mouth; hence, the mouth itself.
11: An ear. From 'azan; broadness. I.e. the ear.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To go straight, go on, advance. Or rasher; a primitive root; to be straight; figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; to duplicate or repeat; by implication, to protest, testify; intensively, to encompass, restore.
12: A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
Poor, afflicted, humble. From anah; depressed, in mind or circumstances.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To help, succor. A primitive root; to surround, i.e. Protect or aid.
13: To perish. A primitive root; properly, to wander away, i.e. Lose oneself; by implication to perish.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
A blessing. From barak; benediction; by implication prosperity.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
14: Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
A robe. From ma'al in the sense of covering; a robe.
A turban. Or tsanowph; or tsaniyphah; from tsanaph; a head-dress.
15: To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Foot. From ragal; a foot; by implication, a step; by euphem. The pudenda.
16: I. Sometimes; a primitive pro. I.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
In want, needy, poor. From 'abah, in the sense of want; destitute.
To search. A primitive root; properly, to penetrate; hence, to examine intimately.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
17: To break, break in pieces. A primitive root; to burst.
Teeth. Contr. From yala'; properly, a biter, i.e. A tooth.
Unjust, unrighteous one. Intensive from aval; evil.
To throw, fling, cast. A primitive root; to throw out, down or away.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
18: To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To expire, perish, die. A primitive root; to breathe out, i.e. expire.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
A nest. Contracted from qanan; a nest, sometimes including the nestlings; figuratively, a chamber or dwelling.
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
19: A root. From sharash; a root.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Night mist, dew. From talal; dew.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
From qatsar; severed, i.e. Harvest, the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb.
20: Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.
A bow. From qashah in the original sense of bending: a bow, for shooting or the iris.
To pass on or away, pass through. A primitive root; properly, to slide by, i.e. to hasten away, pass on, spring up, pierce or change.
21: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To wait, await. A primitive root; to wait; by implication, to be patient, hope.
A prim root; to be dumb; by implication, to be astonished, to stop; also to perish.
To or foreign. A prol. And separable form of the prepositional prefix; to or for.
Counsel, advice. From ya'ats; advice; by implication, plan; also prudence.
22: The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To repeat, do again. A primitive root; to fold, i.e. Duplicate; by implication, to transmute.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A word, speech, utterance. From malal

23: To wait, await. A primitive root; to wait; by implication, to be patient, hope.
To open wide (the mouth). A primitive root; to yawn, i.e. Open wide.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
Latter rain, spring rain. From laqash; the spring rain; figuratively, eloquence.
24: To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
25: To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.

Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.

A band, troop. From gadad; a crowd.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.

Want, poverty. From chacer; lack; hence, destitution.

Broom plant, retem. Or rothem; from ratham; the Spanish broom.

If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!

26: To rain. A primitive root; to rain.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
27: To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Perhaps a ravage. Or showtah; or shoah; from an unused root meaning to rush over; a tempest; by implication, devastation.
Desolation. Or mshoah; from the same as show'; ruin, abstractly or concretely.
To sprout, spring up. A primitive root; to sprout.
A place or act of going forth, issue, export, source, spring.
Grass. From dasha; a sprout; by analogy, grass.
28: Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Or. Presumed to be the constructive or genitival form of -av; short for 'avvah; desire; hence or, also if.

Night mist, dew. From talal; dew.
29: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
Frost, ice. Or qorach; from qarach; ice; hence, hail; by resemblance, rock crystal.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.

A cover, a tankard, white frost. From kaphar; properly, a cover, i.e. a tankard; also white frost.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
30: Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To withdraw, hide. A primitive root; to secrete.
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
To capture, seize, take. A primitive root; to catch; generally, to capture or occupy; also to choose; figuratively, to cohere.
31: To bind, league together, conspire. A primitive root: to tie, physically or mentally.
A bond, group. By transitive from anad; a bond, i.e. Group.
A cluster of stars. From the same as kuwmaz; a cluster of stars, i.e. The Pleiades.
Or. Presumed to be the constructive or genitival form of -av; short for 'avvah; desire; hence or, also if.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
A cord. Act participle feminine of mashak; something drawing, i.e. a cord.
A heavenly constellation. The same as kciyl; any notable constellation; specifically Orion.
32: A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Apparently from nazar in the sense of distinction; some noted constellation, perhaps collectively, the zodiac.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
To lead, guide. A primitive root; to guide; by implication, to transport.
(a constellation) perhaps Great Bear. Or Ash; from uwsh; the constellation of the Great Bear.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
33: A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Something prescribed, an enactment, statute. Feminine of choq, and meaning substantially the same.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
34: To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Abundance, quantity. Feminine of shepha'; copiousness.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To cover. A primitive root; properly, to plump, i.e. Fill up hollows; by implication, to cover.
35: To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Lightning. From baraq; lightning; by analogy, a gleam; concretely, a flashing sword.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
36: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To put, set. A primitive root; to place.
Inward parts. From tachah in the sense of overlaying; the kidneys; hence the inmost thought.
Or. Presumed to be the constructive or genitival form of -av; short for 'avvah; desire; hence or, also if.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
Perhaps appearance, phenomenon. From the same as Sekuw; observant, i.e. the mind.
37: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
Dust, cloud. From shachaq; a powder: by analogy, a thin vapor; by extension, the firmament.

To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
A skin-bag for, liquids, a vase, a lyre. Or nebel; from nabel; a skin-bag for liquids; hence, a vase; also a lyre.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
38: Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
A casting. From agiyl; properly, fusion, i.e. Literally, a casting; figuratively, a mass.
A clod (of earth). From an unused root meaning to pile together; a lump of clay.
To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
39: To hunt. A primitive root; to lie alongside; by implication, to catch an animal; to victual.
Prey, food, a leaf. From taraph; something torn, i.e. A fragment, e.g. A fresh leaf, prey, food.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Young lion. From kaphar; a village; also a young lion.
40: To bow, be bowed down, crouch. A primitive root; to sink or depress.
Habitation. Or msonah; feminine of ma'own, and meaning the same.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
To or foreign. A prol. And separable form of the prepositional prefix; to or for.
A lying in wait, a covert, lair. From 'arab; ambuscade.
A thicket, booth. Fem of cok; a hut or lair.
41: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
The chase, game, lunch. From a form of tsuwd and meaning the same; the chase; also game; lunch.
A raven. Or mowreb; from arab; a raven.
Child, son, boy, youth. From yalad; something born, i.e. A lad or offspring.
To cry out (for help). A primitive root; properly, to be free; but used only causatively and reflexively, to halloo.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To err. A primitive root; to vacillate, i.e. Reel or stray; also causative of both.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To or foreign. A prol. And separable form of the prepositional prefix; to or for.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
5: To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Two (a card. number). Dual of sheniy; feminine shttayim; two; also twofold.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
6: The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
A patriarch. From 'ayab; hated; Ijob, the patriarch famous for his patience.
From. Or minniy; or minney; for men; properly, a part of; hence, from or out of in many senses.
A hurricane. Or ctarah; from ca'ar; a hurricane.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
7: To gird, encompass, equip. A primitive root; to belt.
Loins. From chalats; only in the dual; the loins.
Man. From gabar; properly, a valiant man or warrior; generally, a person simply.
To ask, inquire. Or shael; a primitive root; to inquire; by implication, to request; by extension, to demand.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
8: Also, yea. A primitive particle; meaning accession; also or yea; adversatively though.
To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
A deed, work. From asah; an action; generally, a transaction; abstractly, activity; by implication, a product or property.
To be wicked, act wickedly. A primitive root; to be wrong; by implication, to disturb, violate.
From anah; properly, heed, i.e. Purpose; used only adverbially, on account of, teleologically, in order that.
To be just or righteous. A primitive root; to be right.
9: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
To thunder. A primitive root; to tumble, i.e. Be violently agitated; specifically, to crash; figuratively, to irritate.
Sound, voice. Or qol; from an unused root meaning to call aloud; a voice or sound.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
10: A primitive root; to advance, i.e. Pass on or continue; causatively, to remove; specifically, to bedeck.
Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
Height. From gabahh; elation, grandeur, arrogance.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
Splendor, majesty, vigor. From an unused root; grandeur.
An ornament, honor, splendor. From hadar; magnificence, i.e. Ornament or splendor.
11: To be dispersed or scattered. A primitive root; to dash in pieces, literally or figuratively.
Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Proud. From ga'ah; lofty; figuratively, arrogant.
To be or become low, to be abased. A primitive root; to depress or sink.
12: To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Proud. From ga'ah; lofty; figuratively, arrogant.
To be humble. A primitive root; properly, to bend the knee; hence, to humiliate, vanquish.
To cast or tread down. A primitive root; to crush with the foot.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
13: To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Dry earth, dust. From aphar; dust; hence, clay, earth, mud.
To bind, bind on, bind up. A primitive root; to wrap firmly; figuratively, to stop, to rule.
To hide, conceal. A primitive root; to hide.
14: Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
To deliver. A primitive root; properly, to be open, wide or free, i.e. to be safe; causatively, to free or succor.
15: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
In form a plural or bhemah, but really a singular of Egyptian derivation; a water-ox, i.e. The hippopotamus or Nile- horse.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Green grass, herbage. Perhaps originally the same as chatsiyr, from the greenness of a courtyard; grass; also a leek.
Cattle, herd, an ox. From baqar; beef cattle or an animal of the ox family of either gender; collectively, a herd.
16: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Or kowach; from an unused root meaning to be firm; vigor, literally or figuratively; also a large lizard.
Loins. From an unused root meaning to be slender; properly, the waist or small of the back; only in plural the loins.
Vigor, wealth. Probably from the same as 'aven; ability, power, wealth.
Sinew, muscle. From sharar in the original sense as in shor; a cord, i.e. sinew.
From an unused root probably meaning to be hollow; the belly, especially the womb; also the bosom or body of anything.
17: Tail, end, stump. From zanab; the tail.
A primitive root; properly, to incline to; by implication to bend; figuratively, to be pleased with, desire.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
A cedar. From 'araz; a cedar tree.
Sinew. Probably from guwd; a thong; by analogy, a tendon.
Thigh. The same as pachad; a testicle.
To be intertwined. A primitive root; to intwine.
18: Bone, substance, self. From atsam; a bone; by extension, the body; figuratively, the substance, i.e. Selfsame.
A channel. From 'acaph; properly, containing, i.e. A tube; also a bed or valley of a stream; also a strong thing or a hero.
Copper, bronze. Or nchushah; feminine of nachuwsh; copper.
A bone, strength. From garam; a bone; hence, self, i.e. very.
A wrought metal rod. From tuwl in the sense of hammering out; an iron bar.
Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
19: Beginning, chief. From the same as ro'sh; the first, in place, time, order or rank.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Way, road, distance, journey, manner. From darak; a road; figuratively, a course of life or mode of action, often adverb.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
20: Mountain, hill, hill country. A shortened form of harar; a mountain or range of hills.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Field, land. Or saday; from an unused root meaning to spread out; a field.
To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
There, thither. A primitive particle; there then; often thither, or thence.
21: To lie down. A primitive root; to lie down.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
(a kind of) lotus. From an unused root meaning to be slender; the lotus tree.
A covering, hiding place, secrecy. Or cithrah,; from cathar; a cover.
A stalk, reed. From qanah; a reed; by resemblance a rod, shaft, tube, stem, the radius, beam.
A swamp. Intensive from bots; a swamp.
22: (a kind of) lotus. From an unused root meaning to be slender; the lotus tree.
Or sakak; a primitive root; properly, to entwine as a screen; by implication, to fence in, cover over, protect.
(a kind of tree) perhaps poplar, also a wadi in Moab. From arab; a willow.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
A primitive root; to revolve, surround, or border; used in various applications, literally and figuratively.
23: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
A stream, river. From nahar; a stream; figuratively, prosperity.
To oppress, wrong, extort. A primitive root; to press upon, i.e. Oppress, defraud, violate, overflow.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be in trepidation, hurry or alarm. A primitive root; properly, to start up suddenly, i.e. to hasten away, to fear.
To trust. A primitive root; properly, to hie for refuge; figuratively, to trust, be confident or sure.
The principal river of Pal. From yarad; a descender; Jarden, the principal river of Palestine.
To burst forth. Or goach; a primitive root; to gush forth, generally to issue.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
24: To take. A primitive root; to take.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To puncture. A primitive root; to puncture, literally or figuratively.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
A bait or lure, a snare. Or moqesh; from yaqosh; a noose: by implication, a hook.

1: To draw, drag. A primitive root; to draw, used in a great variety of applications.
From lavah; a wreathed animal, i.e. A serpent; figuratively, the constellation of the dragon; also as a symbol of Bab.
A hook, fishhook. Probably from chakah; a hook.
Tongue. Or lashon; also feminine lshonah; from lashan; the tongue, used literally, and figuratively.
(abbreviated Am. 8:8); a primitive root; to subside; by implication, to be overflowed, cease; causatively, to abate, subdue.
A rope, a measuring line, a district, inheritance, a noose, a company, a throe, ruin.
2: To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A rush, bulrush. From the same as 'agam; a marshy pool; by implication a rush; collectively a rope of rushes.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
To puncture. A primitive root; to puncture, literally or figuratively.
Jaw, cheek. From an unused root meaning to be soft; the cheek; hence, the jaw-bone.
Briar, bramble, hook, ring, fetter. From an unused root apparently meaning to pierce; a thorn; by analogy, a ring for the nose.
3: To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Supplication for favor. Or tachanuwnah; from chanan; earnest prayer.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Tender, delicate, soft. From rakak; tender; by implication, weak.
4: To cut off, cut down. A primitive root; to cut; by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant.
A covenant. From barah (like bara'); a compact.
From amam; adverb or preposition, with, in varied applications; specifically, equally with; often with prepositional prefix.
To take. A primitive root; to take.
Slave, servant. From abad; a servant.
5: To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
A bird. Or tsippor; from tsaphar; a little bird.
or put him on a leash
To bind, league together, conspire. A primitive root: to tie, physically or mentally.
A girl, maiden. Feminine of na'ar; a girl.
6: Associate, partner (in a trade). From chabar; a partner.
To trade, get by trade. Usually assigned as a primitive root, but probably only a special application of karah; to purchase.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To divide. A primitive root); to cut or split in two; to halve.
Canaanite -- inhabitant of Canaan. Patrial from Kna'an; a Kenaanite or inhabitant of Kenaan; by implication, a pedlar.
7: To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
A skin. From uwr; skin; by implication, hide, leather.
A barb, spear. Feminine of sok in the sense of sek; a dart.
Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.
A fish. Or dag; from dagah; a fish; or perhaps rather from da'ag; but still better from da'ag; a fish.
From tsalal reduplicated; a clatter, i.e. whirring; a cricket; also a harpoon, a cymbal.
8: To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Remember. A primitive root; properly, to mark, i.e. To remember; by implication, to mention; also to be male.
A battle, war. From lacham; a battle; generally, war.
Not (a subjective neg.). A negative particle; not; once as a noun, nothing.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
9: Lo! behold! A primitive particle; lo!; also if.
To lie, be a liar. A primitive root; to lie, literally or figuratively.
Sight, appearance, vision. From ra'ah; a view; also an appearance, whether a shape, or a vision.
To hurl, cast. A primitive root; to pitch over or reel; hence to cast down or out.
10: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Cruel, fierce. From an unused root; violent; by implication deadly; also brave.
To rouse oneself, awake. A primitive root; to wake.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To set or station oneself, take one's stand. A primitive root; to place; reflexively, to station, offer, continue.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
11: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To come or be in front, meet. A primitive root; to project, i.e. Precede; hence, to anticipate, hasten, meet.
A primitive root; to be safe; figuratively, to be completed; by implication, to be friendly; by extension, to reciprocate.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
12: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To cut in, engrave, plow, devise.
Empty, idle talk. From bada'; a brag or lie; also a liar.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
Strength, might. Feminine passive participle from the same as gibbowr; force; by implication, valor, victory.
Perhaps beauty. Another form for chen; beauty.
An order, row, estimate. From arak; a pile, equipment, estimate.
13: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To uncover, remove. A primitive root; to denude; by implication, to exile; figuratively, to reveal.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
A garment, clothing, raiment. Or lbush; from labash; a garment; by implication A wife.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
The double. From kaphal; a duplicate.
A halter, jaw. From an unused root meaning to curb; a halter; by implication, the jaw.
14: An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To open wide, to loosen, begin, plough, carve. A primitive root; to open wide; specifically, to loosen, begin, plough, carve.
A door. From dalah; something swinging, i.e. The valve of a door.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Circuit, round about. Or cbiybah; from cabab; a circle, neighbour, or environs; but chiefly around.
Terror, dread. Or remah; from the same as 'ayom; fright; concrete, an idol.
Tooth, ivory. From shanan; a tooth; specifically ivory; figuratively, a cliff.
15: A channel. From 'acaph; properly, containing, i.e. A tube; also a bed or valley of a stream; also a strong thing or a hero.
A shield. Also feminine mginnah; from ganan; a shield; figuratively, a protector; also the scaly hide of the crocodile.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
To shut, close. A primitive root; to shut up; figuratively, to surrender.
A seal, signet ring. Or chotham; from chatham; a signature-ring.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
16: One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
17: To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
To capture, seize, take. A primitive root; to catch; generally, to capture or occupy; also to choose; figuratively, to cohere.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To divide. A primitive root; to break through, i.e. Spread or separate.
18: A sneezing. From an unused root meaning to sneeze; sneezing.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
Eyelid. From uwph; an eyelash; figuratively, morning ray.
19: A torch. Or lappid; from an unused root probably meaning to shine; a flambeau, lamp or flame.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
A spark. From the same as kiyd; properly, something struck off, i.e. A spark.
A primitive root; properly, to be smooth, i.e. to escape; causatively, to release or rescue; specifically, to bring forth young, emit sparks.
20: Smoke. From ashan; smoke, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
A nostril. From the same as nachar; a nostril.
To breathe, blow. A primitive root; to puff, in various applications.
A pot, jar. From the same as dowd; a pot; also a basket.
A rush, bulrush. From the same as 'agam; a marshy pool; by implication a rush; collectively a rope of rushes.
21: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
Coal. Or gacheleth; from an unused root meaning to glow or kindle; an ember.
To blaze up, flame. A primitive root; properly, to lick, i.e. to blaze.
Flame, blade. From an usused root meaning to gleam; a flash; figuratively, a sharply polished blade or point of a weapon.
A primitive root; to go out, in a great variety of applications, literally and figuratively, direct and proxim.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
22: Strength, might. Or rowz; from azaz; strength in various applications.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
Or tsavvar; or tsavvaron; or tsavva.rah; intensively from tsuwr in the sense of binding; the back of the neck.
Faintness, dismay. From da'ab; properly, pining; by analogy, fear.
To spring, leap, dance. A primitive root; to leap.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
23: Refuse, hanging parts. From naphal; a falling off, i.e. Chaff; also something pendulous, i.e. A flap.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
To cling, cleave, keep close. A primitive root; properly, to impinge, i.e. Cling or adhere; figuratively, to catch by pursuit.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. From balah; properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually not at all; also lest.
To totter, shake, slip. A primitive root; to waver; by implication, to slip, shake, fall.
24: A form of lebab; the heart; also used very widely for the feelings, the will and even the intellect; likewise for the centre of anything.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
Like, as, when. Or kamow; a form of the prefix k-, but used separately; as, thus, so.
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
A primitive root; properly, to pour out; by implication, to melt or cast as metal; by extension, to place firmly, to stiffen or grow hard.
Lower, lowest. From tachath; lowermost; as noun the depths.
Cleavage, a millstone. From palach; a slice.
25: From nasa'; an elevation or leprous scab; figuratively, elation or cheerfulness; exaltation in rank or character.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
A primitive root; properly, to turn aside from the road, i.e. Sojourn; also to shrink, fear; also to gather for hostility.
A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.
Or sheber; from shabar; a fracture, figuratively, ruin; specifically, a solution.
26: A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
To reach, overtake. A primitive root; to reach.
From balah; properly, failure, i.e. Nothing or destruction; usually without, not yet, because not, as long as, etc.
To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.
A spear. From chanah; a lance.
A quarry or quarrying. From naca' in the sense of projecting; a missile; also a quarry.
Body armor. Or shiryon; and shiryan; also shiryah; and shiryonah; from sharah in the original sense of turning; a corslet.
27: Iron. Perhaps from the root of Birzowth; iron; by extension, an iron implement.
Straw. Probably from banah; properly, material, i.e. refuse haum or stalks of grain.
Copper, bronze. Or nchushah; feminine of nachuwsh; copper.
Tree, trees, wood. From atsah; a tree; hence, wood.
28: Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A bow. From qashah in the original sense of bending: a bow, for shooting or the iris.
To go through, flee. A primitive root; to bolt, i.e. Figuratively, to flee suddenly.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
A stone. From the root of banah through the meaning to build; a stone.
A sling, a, screen, the valve. From qala'; a sling; also a screen, or the valve itself.
To turn, overturn. A primitive root; to turn about or over; by implication, to change, overturn, return, pervert.
29: (a weapon) perhaps club, mace. From an unused root meaning to smite; a club.
To laugh. A primitive root; to laugh; by implication, to play.
A quaking, shaking. From ra'ash; vibration, bounding, uproar.
A dart, javelin. From the same as kiyd; properly, something to strike with, i.e. A dart.
30: Underneath, below, instead of. From the same as Towach; the bottom; only adverbially, below, in lieu of, etc.
Sharpened, sharp, pointed. From chadad; a point.
Earthenware, earthen vessel, sherd, potsherd. A collateral form mediating between cherec and cheresh; a piece of pottery.
To spread. A primitive root; to spread; by implication, to refresh.
Mud, mire, clay. From an unused root meaning apparently to be sticky; mud or clay; figuratively, calamity.
Incised, incisive, a trench, gold, a threshing-sledge, determination, eager.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
31: Depth, deep. Or mtsolah; also mtsuwlah; or mtsulah; from the same as tsuwlah; a deep place.
To boil. A primitive root; to boil.
Or ciyrah; or cirah; from a primitive root meaning to boil up; a pot; also a thorn; by implication, a hook.
To put, place, set. Or siym; a primitive root; to put.
A sea or large body of water; specifically, the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river.
An ointment pot. Feminine of merqach; abstractly, a seasoning; concretely, an unguent-kettle.
32: To be or become light. A primitive root; to be luminous.
Path, pathway. Or nthiybah; or nthibah; from an unused root meaning to tramp; a track.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
Deep, sea, abyss. Or thom; from huwm; an abyss, especially the deep.
Hoary head, old age. Feminine of seyb; old age.
33: As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
